postData.contentHtml:
Sky News recently interrupted its regular programming for a breaking update on the much-anticipated 'Andrew Files,' a 31-page document offering a detailed look into Prince Andrew's role as a UK trade envoy. The revelations are proving to be quite significant for the Royal Family, bringing to light previously unaddressed concerns.

The files, analyzed live on air by Sky News's deputy political editor Sam Coates, reveal a series of 'red flags' raised by senior officials. Among the most notable concerns was a specific instruction that the Duke of York 'should not be offered golfing functions abroad' as it was deemed a 'private activity' that could lead to conflicts of interest. The documents also contained a pre-prepared denial regarding a reputed £100,000 demand for Andrew's office expenses, with officials told to state that 'only expenses incurred in the role would be paid, by agreement with Buckingham Palace.' This suggests a degree of internal worry about financial transparency and accountability even at the time of his appointment.

Perhaps one of the most striking disclosures is the explicit detail that the late Queen Elizabeth II was 'very keen' for Prince Andrew to take on this prominent role. The documents indicate that the Queen saw this as a perfect opportunity for Andrew, whose active naval career was concluding, to contribute to the 'promotion of national interests.' This highlights the monarch's direct involvement and desire to elevate her son's public profile within this capacity.

These findings have been corroborated by various news outlets, all emphasizing the significance of these historic documents and the Queen's push for Andrew's role. While social media sentiment regarding Prince Andrew and the Royal Family is currently categorized as 'mostly positive, some debate,' the release of these files is undoubtedly fueling further discussions and scrutiny around the Duke of York's past activities and their implications for the monarchy.
postData.title: The 'Andrew Files': Unpacking Prince Andrew's Trade Envoy Role and Royal Concerns
postData.summary: Recently released 'Andrew Files' reveal new details about Prince Andrew's controversial tenure as a UK trade envoy, highlighting official 'red flags' and the late Queen Elizabeth II's strong desire for his involvement in promoting national interests. The documents shed light on financial concerns and potential conflicts of interest, sparking renewed public discussion.

sources.title: New Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor files reveal late Queen pushed for son's trade envoy role
Queen pushed for Andrew to get prominent trade role, files reveal
Sky News halted for breaking Andrew update and it's awful for royals
Late queen was ‘very keen’ Andrew had ‘prominent role’ promoting UK interests
Late queen was 'very keen' ex-prince Andrew had role promoting UK interests
